strengths: 15+ years of genuine full-stack engineering across web, mobile, cloud, and embedded systems, with direct TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, and AWS CDK experience at Optilyz and re:cinq — substantially exceeding the 6-year minimum.

Currently building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP (Model Context Protocol) code generators at re:cinq, directly and concretely satisfying the AI agent development requirement at a time when few candidates have production-level exposure to agentic workflows.

Demonstrated deep testing culture: created open-source test runner (Trial) and assertion library (Fluent-Asserts), ran public TDD and refactoring workshops, and applied TDD, pairing, and trunk-based development in production at RIDE Capital.
concerns: Nest.js — the team's primary backend framework — is absent from the resume; candidate may require onboarding time to reach full productivity in that specific framework.
Kotlin and Python are listed as required proficiencies but do not appear in the skills section or any employment history entry.
No explicit mention of Vitest or Playwright; while the candidate has a strong general testing background, familiarity with the team's exact toolchain is unconfirmed.
response.justification: Bogdan brings over 15 years of genuine full-stack experience, with recent roles that map closely to the required stack — TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, and AWS CDK at Optilyz and re:cinq. His current position adds a dimension few candidates can match: hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work including MCP code generators and agentic-workflow frameworks, directly satisfying the AI agent development requirement. A sustained commitment to testing craftsmanship — through open-source tooling, public workshops, and in-production TDD practices — signals the engineering discipline and quality focus the role demands. The primary gap, Nest.js, is a framework a developer of his seniority and Node.js depth can reasonably close in short order, making him a strong fit for the role.
